skill.name: excel-formula-wizard
skill.description: Describe your Excel needs in plain language and get ready-to-use formulas, VBA macros, and pivot table solutions.
skill.summary: Generate Excel formulas, VBA macros, and pivot table solutions from natural language descriptions.
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged two high-severity patterns that are both false positives. The 'weak cryptographic algorithm' alert at line 4 is a misidentification of the Chinese word '公式' (meaning 'formula') in the skill description. The 'high file entropy' alert at line 1 is caused by UTF-8 encoded Chinese text, which naturally produces higher byte-level entropy than ASCII. The skill contains only descriptive markdown with no executable code, network requests, or file system operations.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at line 4 of SKILL.md. This is a false positive caused by Chinese characters ('公式' meaning 'formula') in the description field. No cryptographic algorithms are present in the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 4 contains only Chinese text describing an Excel formula tool. The term '公式' (formula) was misidentified as a cryptographic reference. Zero malicious intent.

low_findings.title: High File Entropy - False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analyzer reported high file entropy (6.25 bits) suggesting possible binary or encrypted content. This is a false positive caused by UTF-8 encoded Chinese characters, which naturally produce higher byte-level entropy than ASCII text. The file is plain markdown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains Chinese text in UTF-8 encoding. Multi-byte CJK characters inherently raise byte entropy above ASCII levels. Content is fully human-readable markdown.

content.user_title: Generate Excel formulas and macros from plain descriptions
content.value_statement: Many people waste hours searching for the right Excel formula or VBA syntax. This skill converts your plain-language needs into working formulas, macros, and pivot table setups in seconds.
actual_capabilities: Convert natural language needs into Excel formulas
Generate VBA macro code for spreadsheet automation
Suggest pivot table configurations for data analysis
Explain formula logic with step-by-step breakdowns
Troubleshoot formula errors and suggest corrections
Recommend the best approach for specific data tasks
limitations: Cannot execute or test formulas inside Excel files directly
May not cover every version-specific Excel feature
Complex business logic may need manual adjustments

use_cases.title: Build financial models with complex lookups
use_cases.description: A financial analyst describes a multi-sheet lookup need and receives VLOOKUP, INDEX/MATCH, or XLOOKUP formulas tailored to their data structure.

use_cases.title: Automate monthly reporting with VBA
use_cases.description: A small business owner describes repetitive formatting and charting tasks and receives a VBA macro that automates the entire workflow.

use_cases.title: Design pivot tables for sales dashboards
use_cases.description: A data analyst describes sales metrics and dimensions and receives a pivot table layout with recommended fields, filters, and value aggregations.

prompt_templates.title: Simple formula request
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a column of dates in column A and sales amounts in column B. I need a formula to calculate the total sales for dates in March 2024.

prompt_templates.title: VBA macro automation
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a VBA macro that loops through all sheets in the workbook, copies the header row from each sheet, and pastes them into a new 'Summary' sheet.

prompt_templates.title: Pivot table configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: I have sales data with columns: Date, Region, Product, Quantity, Revenue. Create a pivot table that shows total revenue by region and product, with a monthly date filter.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced nested formula with conditions
prompt_templates.prompt: I need a single formula that checks if a cell contains a valid email format, returns the domain if valid, and marks it as 'INVALID' otherwise. Include error handling for empty cells.

output_examples.input: Calculate a 10% bonus for employees who exceeded their sales target in column D, otherwise show zero.
output_examples.output:
Use this formula in the bonus column:

=IF(D2>TARGET, D2*0.1, 0)

Replace TARGET with your actual target cell reference or value. Drag the fill handle down to apply to all rows.

output_examples.input: Create a macro that saves the current workbook with today's date in the filename.
output_examples.output:
Here is a VBA macro that saves the workbook with today's date:

Sub SaveWithDate()
    Dim fileName As String
    fileName = "Report_" & Format(Date, "yyyy-mm-dd") & ".xlsx"
    ActiveWorkbook.SaveAs fileName
    MsgBox "Saved as: " & fileName
End Sub

Run this macro from the Developer tab or assign it to a button.

output_examples.input: I need a pivot table from my sales data to see total revenue by region and month.
output_examples.output:
Recommended pivot table layout:

• Rows: Region, then Date (grouped by Month)
• Columns: (none)
• Values: Sum of Revenue
• Filters: Product category (optional)

Steps in Excel:
1. Select your data range
2. Insert → PivotTable
3. Drag 'Region' to Rows
4. Drag 'Date' to Rows, then right-click and group by Month
5. Drag 'Revenue' to Values and ensure it is set to Sum

best_practices: Describe your data layout clearly including column names and ranges
Mention your Excel version if you need backward-compatible formulas
Always test generated formulas on a copy of your data first
anti_patterns: Do not share sensitive financial data in your prompts
Avoid vague requests like 'fix my spreadsheet' without context
Do not run VBA macros from untrusted sources without review

faq.question: Which Excel versions does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill generates formulas compatible with Excel 2016 and newer. It defaults to widely supported functions and can adapt to older versions when you specify your Excel version.

faq.question: Can the skill edit my existing Excel files?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates formulas and macros as text. You must copy and paste them into Excel yourself. This protects your files and gives you full control.

faq.question: Does it support Google Sheets formulas?
faq.answer: Yes. When you mention Google Sheets in your request, the skill will generate formulas using Google Sheets syntax and functions instead of Excel.

faq.question: Can I get an explanation of how a formula works?
faq.answer: Yes. Ask the skill to explain the formula step by step. It will break down each function and show how the parts connect.

faq.question: What if the formula returns an error?
faq.answer: Share the error message and describe your data. The skill can diagnose common errors like #VALUE!, #REF!, and #N/A and suggest corrected formulas.

faq.question: Is VBA macro code safe to run?
faq.answer: The skill generates VBA code for your specific request. Always review the code before running it. Test on a copy of your workbook first. The skill cannot execute code in your files.
